sizing_rule.note: Position Size = Account Equity / (Maximum Loss per Trade / Optimal f). Optimal f is derived from historical trade results and represents the mathematically optimal fraction of capital to risk. The formula relates account size, largest historical loss, and optimal f multiplier.

source_rule_text: Position size is calculated by dividing account equity by the ratio of maximum loss per trade to optimal f, demonstrating the relationship between account size, risk per trade, and position sizing multiplier.
